items.situation: two friends discussing whether to join a campus club or intramural team

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you heard they're starting an intramural volleyball team? Sign-ups close this Friday.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I saw the flyer. But I've also been thinking about joining the photography club — they meet Thursday nights.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Both sound fun. The volleyball team only practices twice a week, though, so it wouldn't eat up all your time.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's true. My schedule's already packed with my chemistry lab. Photography meets every week, plus weekend shoots.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Then the team might fit better. And you'd get to know more people in the building.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good point. I think I'll go with the volleyball. I'll sign up before Friday.

questions.stem: When do sign-ups for the volleyball team close?

options.D: At the end of the term

questions.explanation: 女生开头就说排球队报名周五截止，所以A正确。B「周四晚」是摄影俱乐部的活动时间，被张冠李戴；C「这个周末」和D「学期末」对话里都没提到，属于似是而非的编造。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.C: Sign up for the volleyball team
options.D: Ask a friend for advice first

questions.explanation: 男生最后明确说「I'll go with the volleyball. I'll sign up before Friday」，所以C正确。A是他考虑后放弃的选项；B「退掉化学实验课」是过度推断，他只说日程满，没打算退课；D「先问朋友」对话里毫无依据。

items.situation: two friends coordinating a weekend beach trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Are you still up for the beach trip Saturday? The forecast says it'll be sunny all day.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely! Should we take the bus, or is someone driving?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Jenna offered to drive, but her car only fits four, and there are six of us going now.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh. Then maybe half of us take the early bus and meet the rest there?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That works. The bus leaves the student center at nine sharp.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll set an alarm so I don't miss it. Should I bring snacks for everyone?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, that'd be great. I'll pack the sunscreen and towels.

questions.stem: What time does the bus leave the student center?
questions.explanation: 女生说「The bus leaves the student center at nine sharp」，所以B正确。A「八点」和C「十点」都是改动了关键时间的干扰项；D「午饭后」对话里没提，属于似是而非的编造。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely bring on the trip?
questions.explanation: 男生主动问「Should I bring snacks for everyone?」女生同意，所以D正确。A防晒和毛巾是女生负责带的，被张冠李戴；B「多余车票」对话没提；C「自己开车」与他们决定坐公交的情节相反。

items.situation: two students planning what cheese and food to buy for a dinner party

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So for Saturday's dinner party, I'm thinking a cheese board plus one warm dish. Does that sound like enough?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: For eight people? We'll need at least three cheeses — something soft, something sharp, and a mild one everybody likes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good call. The cheese shop on Elm Street lets you taste samples before you buy.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. But their prices are steep. Let's grab the crackers and fruit from the regular grocery store instead.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Smart. Let's split it — you handle the cheese, and I'll get everything else?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Deal. I'll head to Elm Street right after my afternoon class.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: Which restaurant to book for Saturday
options.B: How to lower the price of the cheese
options.C: What food to buy for their dinner party
options.D: Whether they should cancel the party

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕周六晚宴要买什么奶酪和食物、如何分工采购，所以C概括最准确。A「订餐厅」偏题，他们是自己办party；B「压低奶酪价格」只是其中一个细节，太狭窄；D「取消聚会」与情节完全相反。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do after his afternoon class?

options.A: Go to the cheese shop on Elm Street
options.B: Buy the crackers and fruit himself
options.C: Cook the warm dish at home

questions.explanation: 男生负责买奶酪，并说「I'll head to Elm Street right after my afternoon class」，所以A正确。B买饼干水果是女生分到的任务；C「在家做热菜」对话没提，属于过度推断；D「打电话确认客人」对话里毫无依据。

items.situation: two friends debating whether a new cafe is worth the price

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that new cafe by the arts building? Everyone keeps raving about it.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I went once. The lattes are amazing, honestly the best I've had. But a single one costs six dollars.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Six dollars? That's almost double what the campus coffee cart charges.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. The drinks are great, but I really can't afford it more than once a week.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. Maybe I'll just go for a treat now and then, not every day.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's the way to do it. Save it for when you really need a pick-me-up.

questions.stem: How much does a latte at the new cafe cost?

options.C: It is free with a sample

questions.explanation: 男生说「a single one costs six dollars」，所以D正确。A「两美元」和B「四美元」都是改动了关键价格的干扰项；C「凭样品免费」对话完全没提，属于编造。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do about the new cafe?

options.C: Buy from the coffee cart every day
options.D: Complain to the cafe about its prices

questions.explanation: 女生说「Maybe I'll just go for a treat now and then, not every day」，即偶尔去一次，所以B正确。A「彻底戒咖啡」是过度推断；C「每天去咖啡车」对话没依据；D「向咖啡馆投诉价格」也毫无根据。

items.situation: two students talking about which elective class to take next term

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you decided on an elective for next term? I'm torn between Intro to Film and World Mythology.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm leaning toward the film class. A friend took it and said the professor screens a movie every week.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds fun. But doesn't Film meet at eight in the morning? I'm useless before nine.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, right, it's an early one. Mythology's at two in the afternoon, though — much easier for you.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: True. And I've always loved myths and legends. Maybe that's the better fit for my schedule.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sounds like your mind's basically made up. Go for the mythology one.

questions.stem: What time does the Intro to Film class meet?
questions.explanation: 女生说「doesn't Film meet at eight in the morning?」男生确认是早课，所以A正确。B「九点」是女生说自己九点前没精神，不是上课时间；C「下午两点」是神话课的时间，被张冠李戴；D「晚上」对话没提。

questions.stem: Which class will the woman most likely take?
questions.explanation: 女生因为神话课时间合适又一向喜欢神话，说它「the better fit」，男生也劝她选神话，所以C正确。A电影课因为早八被她排除；B「两门都选」是过度推断；D「一门都不选」与她已倾向选课的情节相反。
